headline: OPmobility announces 541-job automotive exterior plant in Rossford — French supplier brings bumpers and tailgates for GM, Stellantis, Ford to I-75 corridor
paragraphs: OPmobility — a French automotive supplier formerly known as Plastic Omnium — announced June 1, alongside Governor Mike DeWine and JobsOhio, that it will build its first Midwest Exterior solutions facility in Rossford, Wood County. The approximately 600,000-square-foot plant is located on the west side of Glenwood Road south of Buck Road, just off I-75. It will produce plastic automotive exterior components — including bumpers and tailgates — for General Motors, Stellantis, and Ford. The announcement is backed by Ohio Tax Credit Authority approval and JobsOhio assistance; the City of Rossford made no direct financial contribution. Construction was already under way at the site at the time of the naming announcement.

The facility is projected to create more than 541 full-time direct jobs with $40 million in annual payroll by 2030, operating across three shifts five to six days per week. Production is targeted for the second half of 2027. An April 2026 announcement had flagged that an unnamed automotive manufacturer was coming to Rossford with 'nearly 1,000' total jobs expected including construction and indirect positions; the June 1 press release from OPmobility identified the company and confirmed the 541+ direct-employment figure. RGP posted the press release on its news hub, confirming regional EDO involvement in the site-selection process.

The Ohio Tax Credit Authority's formal incentive disclosure confirms the full public-investment picture: OPmobility was approved for a 1.968%, 12-year Job Creation Tax Credit — a rate consistent with a competitively recruited large-employer project. No formal groundbreaking ceremony has been announced as of June 7; construction at the Glenwood Road site was already active at the time of the June 1 naming announcement, and production remains targeted for the second half of 2027.
why_it_matters: OPmobility is Europe's largest dedicated automotive exterior supplier — a company with roughly $9 billion in global revenue that has chosen the Toledo MSA's I-75 corridor for its first Midwest exterior plant. The 541 direct jobs and $40M payroll are the headline, but the supplier-network signal is equally important: bumpers and tailgates for GM, Stellantis, and Ford means OPmobility has locked in long-term vehicle-program contracts running through the 2020s production cycle, anchoring northwest Ohio's Tier-1 supply chain through the EV-transition period. The Rossford location is immediately south of Toledo on I-75 — the same labor market, same supplier ecosystem. The OTC disclosure resolves the public-investment transparency question; watch for the formal groundbreaking date and Tier-2 supplier ripple announcements from RGP.
